title: Poppi Manifest Entry gitContext extension (v1, additive)
description: STRICTLY ADDITIVE extension to the 001 Upload Manifest (https://poppi.app/schemas/v1/manifest.json) ManifestEntry. Adds one OPTIONAL property, 'gitContext', carrying the opt-in, credential-stripped, forge-agnostic git coordinate the cloud uses to link a session to a pull request (poppi/005 FR-024). Present only when 'poppi upload --link-prs' was active AND the session's git context resolved. No existing 001 field is renamed, removed, or made required; additionalProperties stays false by ADDING this property to ManifestEntry, not by relaxing the constraint (poppi-cli 005 FR-010, SC-006). The git context is manifest METADATA: it is NOT part of the redacted session payload and NOT part of the contentHash the incremental ledger keys on (FR-011, SC-007). This fragment is the spec-side public contract; the wire field name on the manifest-create request body is the snake_case 'git_context' (matching 001's 'session_id'/'format_version').
$comment: This file documents the delta against contracts/../001-cli-ingest-client/contracts/manifest.schema.json. When 001's manifest.schema.json is next revised, fold the 'gitContext' property below into its $defs.ManifestEntry.properties so the two stay in sync. The merge is purely additive.

GitContext.description: Forge-agnostic git coordinate for one session. Credential-free and path-free by construction (poppi-cli 005 FR-005/FR-015). Either fully present (repository + commitSha required) or the whole gitContext is absent (FR-008): there is no half-resolved git context.

repository.description: Host + owner/name, derived from the 'origin' remote with any embedded credential stripped before recording (FR-005). Forge-agnostic (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, self-hosted); the cloud decides which hosts it can match a PR for (poppi/005 is GitHub-first).

host.description: Remote host only, e.g. 'github.com'. Never contains userinfo (no '@'), scheme, path, or whitespace. A token can never appear here (SC-003).

owner.description: Repository owner / org, e.g. 'acme'. No '/', no '@', no whitespace.

name.description: Repository name with any trailing '.git' stripped, e.g. 'widgets'.

branch.description: The branch the session was worked on. Prefers the source-recorded work-time branch (FR-006); falls back to the repo's current branch at upload time. OMITTED entirely on detached HEAD (the commitSha still allows PR matching). Sent verbatim when present; may embed a ticket id or codename — this is the user's consented opt-in data (FR-006, spec Edge Cases).

commitSha.description: Full 40-hex SHA of the working directory's HEAD at upload time (FR-007). Valid even for shallow/grafted clones.

ManifestEntryWithGitContext.description: The 001 ManifestEntry with the additive optional gitContext. Shown here in full only to make the additive merge unambiguous; the authoritative base remains 001 manifest.schema.json $defs.ManifestEntry.

contentHash.description: sha256 of the REDACTED PAYLOAD bytes only. gitContext is NOT included in this hash (FR-011, SC-007), so toggling --link-prs does not reclassify an unchanged session.

gitContext.description: OPTIONAL, ADDITIVE (FR-010). Absent on every entry produced without --link-prs and on any entry whose git context could not be resolved (FR-008). A 001-era consumer that ignores this property processes the manifest identically (SC-006).

allOf.$comment: Validation entry point: a single ManifestEntry that MAY carry gitContext.
